relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Job answered Eliphaz from inside the storm of pain.
He wanted his grief weighed honestly.
Its heaviness explained the rashness of his speech.
He felt pierced by Shaddai and surrounded by divine terrors.
Creatures do not cry out without cause.
What is tasteless cannot nourish without salt.
His soul recoiled from what was offered to him.
His hope had narrowed to one request.
He wanted the Divine to end the suffering.
Even then, he would cling to having not denied the Holy One’s words.
He saw no strength left for waiting.
His body was not stone or bronze.
Help and wisdom felt driven away from him.
A fainting friend is owed covenant-kindness, even when faith is fraying.
His friends had become like seasonal streams that fail.
They looked dark and full while cold hid their emptiness.
In heat, they vanished.
Their paths turned into emptiness.
Travelers hoped for water.
Their trust ended in shame.
Job’s friends saw terror and became afraid.
He had not asked them for wealth.
He had not asked them for ransom from enemies.
He asked for real teaching if he had truly strayed.
Upright words have force, but their arguments proved nothing.
They were correcting wind, the speech of despair.
Their counsel felt like bargaining over the vulnerable.
He asked them to face him and believe he was not lying.
He asked them to turn from injustice toward his still-present righteousness.
He insisted his tongue could still discern what was wrong.
